---
title: "A Course for Collaborative Ecologists"
---

<p align="center">

<img src="images/eco-data-synth-photo-collage.png" alt="Photo collage of people meeting at a long table, peple conducting fieldwork, some specimen jars, a snippet of code, some fish, and a small mammal" width="100%"/>

</p>


{{< include workshops/2026-07-27_workshop/index.qmd >}}

## Note on Course Materials

This short course has been offered multiple times, with some variations, at [Ecological Society of America annual meetings](https://esa.orghttps://esa.org). See the [workshops listing](workshops/posts.qmd) for details. We've chosen to assemble these materials as a living website so that we can revisit and improve the materials over time. So, **we recommend that you save the link to this site** so that you can have easy access to these materials now and as they are refined going forward. Also, much of the content is covered more completely in our year-long synthesis course: [Synthesis Skills for Early Career Researchers (SSECR)](https://lter.github.io/ssecr/).

If you are a {{< fa brands github >}} GitHub aficionado, we have deployed this website via GitHub Pages so you could also "star" the website's repository. Simply click the {{< fa brands github >}} GitHub octocat logo on the right side of the navbar (at the top of the screen) to be redirected to the GitHub repository underpinning this website.

Finally, we have developed all of this website using [Quarto](https://quarto.org/).

## Credits

The course and its content were developed by a large team. See the [**People page**](people.qmd) to learn more.

**Supported by:**

::: {#credit-orgs layout-ncol=3}

[![](images/edi-logo.png){width=1.2in}](https://edirepository.org){fig-alt="Environmental Data Initiative (EDI) logo"}

[![](images/LTER-network-logo.png){width=1.2in}](https://lternet.edu){fig-alt="Long Term Ecological Research (LTER) Network Office logo"}

[![](images/NEON-NSF-logo.png)](https://neonscience.org){fig-alt="National Ecological Observatory Network (NEON) logo"}

:::

**Collage photo credits:** Jacob Bøtter via Flickr, CC BY-SA 2.0 \| Jeremy Yoder via Flickr, CC BY-SA 2.0 \| Marco Pfeiffer, CC BY-SA 4.0 \| Gabriel De La Rosa, CC BY-SA 4.0 \| Weecology lab CC BY 4.0 \| NEON (National Ecological Observatory Network)
